event: standard induction treatment consisting of 2 cycles of chemotherapy according to AML-BFM guidelines
admitted to the pediatric intensive care unit
transthoracic echocardiography: severely impaired left ventricular ejection fraction (LVEF) 12% without dilatation of the left ventricle (LVIDd 38 mm)
electrocardiogram: sinus tachycardia with incomplete right bundle branch block
N-terminal prohormone of brain natriuretic peptide (NT-proBNP) 19 384 pg/ml
va-ECMO initiated via cannulation of the left femoral artery and vein
21 Fr/55 cm venous HLS Cannula placed
9 Fr sheath placed into the left superficial femoral artery for distal leg perfusion
diameter of the artery about 3–4 mm
va-ECMO blood flow 3.3 l/min at 3800 rpm
extracorporeal blood flow could not be increased further since pre-oxygenator membrane pressure >350 torr
anti-infective therapy continued with dose-adjustment of meropenem
anti-infective therapy continued with dose-adjustment of ciprofloxacin
mean arterial pressure declined to 40 torr
acute ischemic injury of the non-cannulated leg
right femoral artery surgically dissected in the groin
insufficient blood inflow due to low cardiac output causing leg ischemia
Dacron conduit sewn on the right femoral artery
second arterial cannula (13 Fr/15 cm) introduced through conduit
second arterial cannula connected to the ECMO circuit per Y-connector
extracorporeal blood flow enhanced to 4–5 l/min
mean arterial pressure maintained at 40–50 torr
sufficient preload within the right atrium and vena cava verified by echocardiography
arterial oxygen partial pressure in right radial artery normal
second venous cannula (21 Fr/55 cm) placed into the left atrium via percutaneous atrioseptostomy
second venous cannula connected to the ECMO circuit by a Y-connector
extracorporeal blood flow 5–6 l/min at 3850–4200 rpm
mean arterial pressure rose from a nadir of 30 torr after acidosis leveled out
left ventricle unloaded with no residual left ventricular function
therapeutic anticoagulation with unfractionated heparin; target partial thrombin time 60–80 s
viral myocarditis excluded by a myocardial biopsy
ECMO cannulas in the left femoral vessels removed
no relevant communication between the left and right atrium
Successful allogenic stem cell transplantation was performed
